word_list.word: a candle loses nothing by lighting another candle
word_list.pronunciation: 蓯 k藞忙nd蓹l l藞u藧z岬粃 n藞蕦胃瑟艐 ba瑟 l藞a瑟删瑟艐 蓯n藞蕦冒蓺 k藞忙nd蓹l

senses.definition: used to imply that helping others or sharing knowledge and resources does not diminish our own abilities or resources, and can lead to mutual benefit and growth

examples.x: The woman shared her knowledge and expertise with her colleagues, knowing that a candle loses nothing by lighting another candle, and that helping others can benefit everyone.

word_list.word: the sun loses nothing by shining into a puddle
word_list.pronunciation: 冒蓹 s藞蕦n l藞u藧z岬粃 n藞蕦胃瑟艐 ba瑟 蕛藞a瑟n瑟艐 藢瑟nt蕣 蓯 p藞蕦d蓹l

senses.definition: used to suggest that sharing one's resources or knowledge with others does not diminish one's own value or worth

examples.x: As a teacher, I believe that the sun loses nothing by shining into a puddle, and I strive to give my students the tools and resources they need to succeed.

word_list.pronunciation: w藢蓻n 蓴藧晒 ju藧 h藞蓻lp s藞蕦mw蕦n 蓴藧晒 s藞蕦mb蓱藧di ju藧 h藞蓻lp 藞蓻v晒瑟w藢蕦n

senses.definition: used to say that one should never stop helping others or being kind to them as one's actions, no matter how small, can make the world a better place for others

word_list.word: you scratch my back and I will scratch yours
word_list.pronunciation: ju藧 sk晒藞忙t蕛 ma瑟 b藞忙k 忙nd 藞a瑟 w瑟l sk晒藞忙t蕛 j藞o藧晒z

senses.definition: used suggest that if one does a favor for someone, they will be more likely to reciprocate that favor in the future

word_list.word: claw me, and I will claw (thee|you)
word_list.pronunciation: kl藞蓴藧 m藢i藧 忙nd a瑟 w瑟l kl藞蓴藧 冒藢i藧 蓴藧晒 ju藧

senses.definition: used to suggest that people may be inclined to respond to aggression or hostility with aggression of their own, leading to a cycle of violence or animosity

examples.x: They say that in this town, it's claw me and I will claw thee, so you better watch your back.

word_list.pronunciation: t藞u藧 瑟n d瑟st晒藞蓻s m藢e瑟ks s藞蓴藧晒o蕣 l藞蓻s

senses.definition: used to suggest that sharing one's troubles with someone who is going through a similar experience can help to ease the burden of sorrow or hardship

examples.x: In the aftermath of a natural disaster, the affected community came together to support each other, demonstrating how two in distress makes sorrow less.

word_list.word: company in misery makes it light
word_list.pronunciation: k藞蕦mp蓹ni 瑟n m藞瑟z蓺晒i m藢e瑟ks 瑟t l藞a瑟t

senses.definition: used to suggest that sharing one's problems with others who are going through similar experiences can make those difficulties easier to bear and provide comfort and support

word_list.pronunciation: 伞晒藞i藧f d瑟v藞a瑟d岬籨 瑟z m藢e瑟d l藞a瑟删蓺

senses.definition: used to suggest that when people come together to share their grief, they can help one another through the difficult period and find a way to cope with the pain

examples.x: My neighbor lost her pet recently, and I offered to listen and provide support; I know from experience that grief divided is made lighter.

word_list.word: company in distress makes trouble less
word_list.pronunciation: k藞蕦mp蓹ni 瑟n d瑟st晒藞蓻s m藢e瑟ks t晒藞蕦b蓹l l藞蓻s

senses.definition: used to imply that facing difficulties together with others can provide comfort and support, and can make the situation more bearable

examples.x: After my grandfather passed away, our family gathered together to share memories and support each other, proving how company in distress makes trouble less.

word_list.word: a (trouble|problem) shared is a trouble halved
word_list.pronunciation: 蓯 t晒藞蕦b蓹l p晒藞蓱藧bl蓹m 蕛藞蓻晒d 瑟z 蓯 t晒藞蕦b蓹l h藞忙vd

senses.definition: used to imply that sharing one's problems or worries with others can help to reduce the emotional burden and make them easier to bear

examples.x: The woman felt overwhelmed by her problems, but after talking to her friend, she realized that a trouble shared is a trouble halved, and felt a sense of relief and comfort.

word_list.word: behind every (great|successful) man (there|) stands a woman
word_list.pronunciation: b瑟ha瑟nd 藞蓻v晒i 伞晒藞e瑟t s蓹ks藞蓻sf蓹l m藞忙n 冒蓻晒 st藞忙ndz 蓯 w藞蕣m蓹n

senses.definition: used to suggest that the success of a man is often due to the support, hard work, and sacrifices of a woman in his life

examples.x: Behind every great man stands a woman who has supported him through thick and thin.
Many believe that behind every successful man stands a woman, silently contributing to his achievements.
Behind every great man stands a woman, whether he acknowledges it or not.
It is often said that behind every successful man there stands a woman, supporting him from the shadows.
The saying, behind every great man there stands a woman, reflects the overlooked influence of women in success stories.

word_list.word: the best helping hand is at the end of your sleeve
word_list.pronunciation: 冒蓹 b藞蓻st h藞蓻lp瑟艐 h藞忙nd 瑟z 忙t 冒瑟 藞蓻nd 蕦v j蕣晒 sl藞i藧v

senses.definition: used to suggest that the most effective way to get things done is by relying on one's own skills, abilities, and resources, rather than depending on others for assistance

examples.x: When I was struggling with a project at work, my colleague reminded me that the best helping hand is at the end of your sleeve and encouraged me to rely on my own skills and abilities.

word_list.word: bring not a bagpipe to a man in trouble
word_list.pronunciation: b晒藞瑟艐 n藢蓱藧删蓹 b藞忙伞pa瑟p t蕣 蓯 m藞忙n 瑟n t晒藞蕦b蓹l

senses.definition: used to imply that one should not be insensitive or thoughtless by bringing up trivial or joyful matters to someone experiencing difficulty or suffering

examples.x: When reaching out to a friend who is going through a difficult time, it's important to be mindful of their emotional state and avoid being overly cheerful or upbeat, as the saying goes, bring not a bagpipe to a man in trouble.

word_list.word: he gives twice who gives quickly
word_list.pronunciation: hi藧 伞藞瑟vz tw藞a瑟s h藢u藧 伞藞瑟vz kw藞瑟kli

senses.definition: used to imply that by giving help or support immediately when it is needed, one can have a greater impact and make a bigger difference

examples.x: The charity organization knows that he gives twice who gives quickly, so they respond immediately to crisis situations to provide help and support to those in need.

word_list.pronunciation: w藞蕦n 伞藞蕣d t藞蓽藧n d瑟z藞蓽藧vz 蓯n藞蕦冒蓺

senses.definition: used to imply that acts of kindness and generosity should be repaid with similar acts, emphasizing the importance of mutual benefit and showing gratitude for the kindness of others

examples.x: After Mary helped her neighbor with groceries and yard work, her neighbor returned the favor by baking her a cake and offering to watch her pets while she went on vacation, proving that one good turn deserves another.

word_list.word: it is (far|way|) better to give than to receive
word_list.pronunciation: 瑟t 瑟z f藞蓱藧晒 w藞e瑟 b藞蓻删蓺 t蓹 伞藞瑟v 冒蓯n t蓹 晒瑟s藞i藧v

senses.definition: used to imply that the act of giving can bring greater joy, satisfaction, and personal growth than simply receiving something

examples.x: Karen decided to donate her time and resources to help the less fortunate in her community because she believes that it is better to give than to receive.

word_list.word: give a man fish and you feed him for a day, teach a man to fish and you feed him for a lifetime
word_list.pronunciation: 伞藞瑟v 蓯 m藞忙n f藞瑟蕛 忙nd ju藧 f藞i藧d h藢瑟m f蓺晒蓹 d藞e瑟 t藞i藧t蕛 蓯 m藞忙n t蓹 f藞瑟蕛 忙nd ju藧 f藞i藧d h藢瑟m f蓺晒蓹 l藞a瑟fta瑟m

senses.definition: used to emphasize that temporary aid may solve immediate needs, but teaching skills and knowledge empowers individuals to become self-sufficient and independent in the long term
